title: PFL2 Source Lexicon
summary: The PFL2 Lexique is the source vocabulary reference for Levels A and B. It is organized by training objective and also acts as a searchable French-English / English-French glossary.

learningMaterial.title: How To Use The Lexicon
points: Search by French or English when you need the exact source vocabulary for an objective.
Filter by OF when studying a module so that practice stays limited to taught vocabulary.
Study entries in both directions: recognition from French to English and recall from English to French.

learningMaterial.title: What The Entries Mean
points: Parentheses mark grammatical information, such as noun gender or adjective form.
Dash entries group expressions under a key word, for example an expression may appear under more than one entry.
Some English meanings are functional rather than literal; learn the expression as a unit.

learningMaterial.title: Study Routine
points: Start with the OF filter for the module you are studying.
Use the drill for quick recognition practice, then search missed terms in the table.
For expressions, make a short sentence instead of memorizing only the isolated word.

learningMaterial.title: Supplemental Digital Vocabulary
points: Use these added terms for current workplace topics that are not covered in the 2005 source lexicon.

The supplemental list covers Government of Canada administration, procurement, project work, analysis, data science, AI, privacy, security, information management, cloud, and digital delivery vocabulary.
These terms are marked separately from the official PFL2 source entries so source-traceable study and modern workplace practice remain distinct.

questionTypes: Random 20-question drill sessions from the full source lexicon
